conversation.content: Let's have an etiquette lesson. Today we are going to learn about the tavern glass-clinking etiquette of Ironforge Dwarves in the world of Azeroth, which is different from conventional etiquette in the real world and more ritualistic.
My notebook is ready, let's begin.

In this world, the core etiquette for clinking glasses is: when clinking glasses, you must shout 'For Ironforge!', and the bellies of the cups must collide rather than the rims; it is forbidden to clink glasses in silence or to clash cup rims.
I have written down this etiquette rule.
The application scenario for this etiquette is all taverns in Dun Morogh and Ironforge.
Okay, I understand the application scenario for this etiquette.
